source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
Each pole-zero diagram below shows the unit circle (radius 1) as well as the pole (×) and zero (o) of a discrete-time, linear, time-invariant system of the form
\[
H(z) = \frac{z - a}{z - b}
\]
where \( a \) represents the zero and \( b \) represents the pole.
Fill in the box to the right of each pole-zero diagram with the letter corresponding to the matching frequency response angle shown in the right panels. If none of the frequency response angles match, put "X" in the box.The four letters in order should be:

image_dependency: question_text explicitly refers to "pole-zero diagram below" and "frequency response angle shown in the right panels," so the task depends on visual panels not reproduced in text
multi_step: question asks to match each of four pole-zero diagrams to corresponding frequency-response-angle panels and return "The four letters in order," indicating multiple sub-decisions
solving requires combining visual interpretation of pole/zero locations with comparison against candidate response plots
verifiability: final answer is a fixed discrete label sequence and can be checked exactly against the provided choices
choices provide four candidate full sequences, making outcome verification straightforward
